content:
- Read progress log and active/done task summaries.
- Found one unblocked failing high-priority bug and two failing non-bug tasks.
- Refresh task summary and read chooser rules next; likely pick the high-priority bug unless blockers appear.

- Refreshed Ralph task summaries and chooser rules; active backlog still has one unblocked failing high-priority bug plus two failing workflow tasks.
- Confirmed repository stack from codebase: Nix-driven Rust workspace with GitHub workflow work; no blockers were visible in current task summary.
- Selected next task by bug-first precedence: .ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-fix-nix-flake-check-without-docker.md. Quit immediately after writing .ralph/current_task.txt.

- Read prior progress, current task pointer, and the required improve-code-boundaries and tdd skill instructions.
- Confirmed active task path is .ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-fix-nix-flake-check-without-docker.md.
- Reading the task file and adjacent task/story directories next to determine whether this turn is planning-only or execution.

- Read the active bug task file.
- Determined the task has no existing execution marker or linked plan, so this turn should create a plan and stop.
- Inspecting other task and plan files now to copy the repository's expected planning format before editing.

- Inspected repository conventions for task plan files and confirmed task files store a <plan> path.
- Planning is still the correct mode for this turn; no implementation started.
- Reading flake.nix, Makefile, and verify/long-test surfaces now so the bug plan targets the actual failing boundaries without running nix flake check yet.

- Identified likely bug-plan hotspots in flake.nix: verify-binary, runner-crate-nextest-long, and docker/image packaging outputs.
- Started reading the long-lane test harness and the recent story-29 image-generation plan to avoid planning against stale architecture.
- Need to map where Docker or image assumptions still survive in tests, workflows, docs, or naming.

- Confirmed the runner long-lane entrypoints are ignored tests in default_bootstrap_long_lane.rs.
- Reading the e2e harness internals now to see whether long-lane failures come from containerized service orchestration, verify binary wiring, or both.
- Need the plan to target the first real failing public behavior, not just whichever file is easiest to edit.

- Found that the current long-lane harness is already host-process based for runner/cockroach/postgres startup; container assumptions are much smaller than the task text suggests.
- Planning focus is shifting toward stale flake/package naming and any remaining image-oriented outputs or test surfaces, plus the Go verify-binary path.
- Reading backlog/task summaries and repo references now for any recorded evidence of the actual current flake-check failures.

- Confirmed the repo-level direction has already moved away from Dockerfile-built runtime orchestration; there is no .github dir in the current checkout.
- I am checking the remaining README and artifact surfaces for stale compose/image assumptions so the plan can include the required cleanup scope.
- I also want the adjacent story-29 task wording in view so this bug plan does not drift across story boundaries.

- The remaining open question is whether compose artifacts are still an intentionally supported public surface or now just stale legacy.
- I am reading the checked-in compose files, current git status, and the story-29 task-05 plan so the bug plan does not accidentally delete a surface another active design still requires.
- After that I will write the bug plan and link it from the task file, then stop.

- Plan structure is ready; I am doing one last search for flake public output names so the planned red/green slices reference the current contracts accurately.
- After that I will create the bug plan file, link it from the task markdown, and stop without starting execution.

- Created and linked the bug execution plan at .ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-fix-nix-flake-check-without-docker_plans/2026-04-28-nix-flake-check-without-docker-plan.md.
- The plan is execution-ready and ends with NOW EXECUTE. It targets the main boundary smell in flake.nix: package/test ownership is muddy around verify-binary and long-lane checks, while some naming/docs still imply older Docker-backed validation paths.
- No implementation or validation commands were started in this turn beyond repository inspection and plan/task-file edits.
- Next turn should read the plan, execute Slice 0 in Nix only, enumerate every failing flake-check component, then take the first real verify-related failure as the first red/green slice.

- Read prior Ralph progress, current task pointer, and the improve-code-boundaries/tdd skill instructions.
- Confirmed the active task is the nix flake check without docker bug and prior work already created a plan ending in NOW EXECUTE.
- Reading the task markdown and linked plan now; next step is to execute the first red/green slice and enumerate actual flake-check failures.

- Read the task markdown and linked NOW EXECUTE plan.
- Confirmed this turn is execution, not planning; starting Slice 0 to enumerate the flake check graph and identify concrete failing components.
- Reading flake/Make surfaces and then running focused Nix commands so the first real failure can become the red tracer bullet.

- Ran the first focused Nix build inventory, but targeted x86_64-linux attrs by mistake on an aarch64-linux machine.
- Every enumerated failure so far is the same platform mismatch, so those are not yet the real task failures.
- Rerunning the inventory against builtins.currentSystem attrs now to identify the actual failing verify and long-lane components.

- Completed the correct aarch64-linux flake inventory.
- Identified current failing components: runner-crate-fmt; runner-crate-nextest-long/test-long with Cockroach rejecting envelope=enriched; molt-go-test/test-molt with rowverify tests dialing 127.0.0.1:5432 without a provisioned Postgres; verify-binary package itself passes.
- Starting the first actual red/green slice now by making runner-crate-fmt green, then I will move to the verify/test-boundary failure and the long-lane failure one by one.

- Made the first verify-boundary code change in flake.nix.
- Added an explicit Nix-owned molt-go test harness that provisions local Postgres plus source/target Cockroach processes before running go test, instead of assuming host daemons.
- Rerunning only the molt-go-test check now to see the next real failure after fixing the missing runtime boundary.

- Verify-related flake slice is green: molt-go-test now passes after provisioning explicit local databases and narrowing the source boundary to the module plus required cert fixtures.
- Added wrapped webhook payload support for runner ingest, keyed by full table topics and positional primary-key arrays, and changed the long-lane changefeed SQL to request full_table_name with the wrapped envelope.
- The new wrapped-payload webhook contract test is green; rerunning the full long-lane attr now to identify the next remaining failure after the envelope shift.

- Verified execution enough to invalidate the original NOW EXECUTE plan.
- Green so far: runner-crate-fmt; explicit molt-go-test derivation after adding Nix-native Postgres/Cockroach runtime and narrowing its source boundary; wrapped webhook payload ingest contract through a new focused runner test.
- Long-lane envelope bug is fixed, but the next blocker is a different runtime prerequisite: local Cockroach rejects CREATE CHANGEFEED because the test environment lacks an enterprise license. I switched the plan file back to TO BE VERIFIED and must stop here so the design can be reconsidered.

- Read prior Ralph progress, current task pointer, and the required tdd/improve-code-boundaries skill instructions.
- Confirmed previous execution discovered a new blocker: local Cockroach rejects CREATE CHANGEFEED without an enterprise license, and prior work switched the plan back to TO BE VERIFIED.
- Reading the active task markdown, linked plan, and improve-code-boundaries skill directory next so I can decide whether this turn is planning-only again.

- Confirmed the linked plan still ends in TO BE VERIFIED, so implementation must not continue in this turn.
- Read the existing plan and skill smell guidance; the new blocker is a licensed Cockroach CDC runtime boundary, not the earlier Docker/envelope issue.
- Gathering exact flake and long-lane code around changefeed creation next so I can revise the plan and capture the relevant boundary smells precisely.

- Confirmed from code that long-lane harnesses still issue CREATE CHANGEFEED directly, while flake.nix pins Cockroach 23.1 and already allows unfree cockroachdb builds.
- Verified against Cockroach Labs docs that sink-backed CREATE CHANGEFEED is the enterprise form, while EXPERIMENTAL CHANGEFEED FOR is the core sinkless form; webhook sinks also require HTTPS.
- Preparing two edits now: revise the execution plan around the licensed CDC boundary, and add a smell-set file capturing the wrong-place flake/test/runtime ownership issues.

- Revised the existing bug plan instead of resuming execution. The plan now explicitly records the official Cockroach product split: sink-backed CREATE CHANGEFEED is enterprise, while EXPERIMENTAL CHANGEFEED FOR is sinkless and not a drop-in replacement for the webhook-based long lane.
- Added a smell-set task at .ralph/tasks/smells/2026-04-28-nix-flake-check-boundary-smells.md capturing the flake/test/runtime wrong-place boundary, mixed long-lane harness responsibilities, and string-built changefeed SQL boundary.
- Next turn should decide the CDC runtime design concretely: either provision a Nix-native licensed Cockroach test runtime for long-lane checks, or re-scope into a broader CDC-contract redesign task before changing code again.

- Read prior progress, current task pointer, the bug task markdown, the linked plan, and the required tdd/improve-code-boundaries skill material.
- Confirmed the active plan still ends in TO BE VERIFIED because the earlier execution hit a Cockroach CDC enterprise-license blocker after fixing flake fmt, molt-go-test, and wrapped webhook payload handling.
- Next I am inspecting the repo for any existing Nix-native Cockroach license/runtime path or alternative CDC contract boundary so I can decide whether to move the plan back to NOW EXECUTE or keep it in verification mode.

- Read the existing TO BE VERIFIED plan, boundary-smell notes, and the current long-lane harness/runtime code.
- Reproduced the real blocker with `nix build -L .#checks.$(nix eval --impure --expr builtins.currentSystem --raw).test-long`: long-lane tests fail at `CREATE CHANGEFEED` with `ERROR: use of CHANGEFEED requires an enterprise license` and `SQLSTATE: XXC02`.
- Verified the flake is currently supplying CockroachDB 23.1.14, while current official Cockroach licensing docs scope the modern single-node no-key behavior to 23.1.29+/23.2.15+/24.1.7+/24.2.5+/24.3.0+; this narrows the blocker to an explicit runtime-version boundary, not a CDC-contract redesign.
- Updated the linked plan at `.ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-fix-nix-flake-check-without-docker_plans/2026-04-28-nix-flake-check-without-docker-plan.md` to choose the concrete execution design: introduce a named `cockroachdb-runtime` boundary, pin it to a post-transition 23.1 patch (target 23.1.30, otherwise newest >=23.1.29), route long-lane and Go harnesses through it, and keep `verify-binary` as a pure package artifact.
- Replaced the plan terminator from TO BE VERIFIED to NOW EXECUTE and stopped without starting implementation, per task instructions.
- Next turn should read the NOW EXECUTE plan and begin the first red/green slice by adding the focused long-lane tracer-bullet test, then making it green via the explicit Cockroach runtime upgrade before rerunning the remaining Nix-backed checks.

- Read previous progress, current task pointer, the bug task markdown, and the linked plan ending in NOW EXECUTE.
- Confirmed the next execution slice is the focused long-lane tracer bullet for Cockroach changefeed creation under the current Nix-supplied runtime.
- Reading the relevant improve-code-boundaries smell notes next, then I will inspect the long-lane tests and flake wiring before making the first red test change.

- Confirmed the active plan is NOW EXECUTE and the current flake still pins CockroachDB 23.1.14 through an ambient `cockroachdb` binding.
- Inspected the long-lane harness and found the clean test boundary: bootstrap drives audited Cockroach SQL, so a focused ignored test can prove initial changefeed creation directly.
- Next I am checking Crane's supported nextest arguments so I can add a focused Nix check attr for that single long-lane tracer-bullet test before changing the runtime version boundary.

- Added the first red test boundary: a bootstrap source audit plus a new ignored long-lane test asserting default bootstrap creates the initial changefeed for `public.customers`.
- The first Nix red run failed before reaching Cockroach because `/` is nearly full; `nix build` ran out of space while unpacking the existing runner nextest cargo artifacts.
- I am inspecting reclaimable scratch/build space now so I can rerun the red test and get back to the actual runtime-version failure instead of a host disk-space blocker.

- The first honest red run was blocked by host disk exhaustion, not by code. I reclaimed 20.7 GiB safely with `nix store gc` and reran the long-lane build.
- While the cache refill rebuild runs, I finished the green-side runtime boundary change in `flake.nix`: replaced the ambient `pkgs.cockroachdb` binding with one explicit Cockroach runtime derivation pinned to v23.1.30 and wired all Nix-native Cockroach surfaces through it.
- Next I need the rerun against the updated flake to reach actual long-lane execution so I can verify the bootstrap tracer bullet goes green and then move to the next remaining failure, if any.

- The first green attempt against v23.1.30 failed at the Nix boundary, not the runtime boundary: I used raw tarball checksums instead of `fetchzip` unpacked hashes.
- Prefetched the correct unpacked hashes for CockroachDB v23.1.30 on both arm64 and amd64 and patched `flake.nix` accordingly.
- The upgraded long-lane check is running again now; next result should tell me whether the bootstrap tracer bullet is green or whether a different real runtime/test failure remains.

- The v23.1.30 runtime upgrade made the first tracer-bullet test green and pushed 17/19 ignored long-lane tests green.
- The next real failures are duplicate/replay feed audits, both panicking because the webhook chaos gateway does not observe source job IDs in wrapped webhook deliveries.
- I am inspecting the gateway/job-id extraction boundary now and will fix one failing behavior first, then rerun the long lane to see whether the sibling replay scenario also goes green.

- Resumed from prior context with long-lane validation still running.
- Next step is to poll make test-long, confirm final gate status, then do task bookkeeping only if all required checks are green.

- Refreshed tdd and improve-code-boundaries skill instructions before final close-out.
- make test-long is still running; previously failing bootstrap and duplicate/replay long-lane cases are now passing.

- make test-long streamed cleanly through 19/19 passing ignored long-lane tests, but the session handle disappeared before shell exit was captured.
- Rerunning make check/make lint/make test/make test-long from the final tree to have explicit command exits for close-out.
